description:
Community and Social Services Career Expo Date:  April 13, 2026 Time:  3:30pm-6:30pm Location:  University Hall Conference Center (7th Floor) 
 Registration is available on Handshake:  https://app.joinhandshake.com/career_fairs/37dfb726-9582-4e23-9c82-6561adbc3e11/student_preview 
   
 Description: 
 Calling all community and service-minded students exploring clinical and non-clinical career options! 
   
 Are you looking for a job or internship? Are you curious about the career paths you can pursue with your major or graduate program? If so, the Community and Social Services Career Expo is an amazing opportunity to meet employers that align with your professional interests.  Co-sponsored by:   College of Humanities and Social Sciences, College for Community Health, Office of Community Engagement and Partnerships, Office of Experiential Education and Career Connections, Office of Government Relations, Office of International Academic Initiatives

title: Art Forum featuring Ginny Casey
description:
The Department of Art & Design presents the Spring 2026 Art Forum series. Art Forum is a speaker series featuring artists, designers, art historians, curators, and art critics from around the world presenting their work and ideas in an open forum.   
   
 The next speaker in this engaging series is painter, Ginny Casey who is known for her surreal, dreamlike compositions. She will discuss her work and share insights into her latest projects. 
   
 Ginny Casey paintings feature decrepit interiors teeming with objects such as chisels, woodcutters, pulleys, and books that are distorted, engorged, disproportioned, and rendered biomorphic. Casey’s environments estrange the domestic, casting shades of anxiety, perversity, and discomfort on everyday items. Architecture in her work follows a dream logic, with trap doors, staircases leading to nowhere, and ladders extending into the abyss. 
   
 Casey’s process begins with drawings of individual objects, which she redraws and collages together to build relationships and narratives. She works intuitively, allowing stories to emerge from her subconscious without preconceived notions of the finished painting. Her practice draws upon psychoanalysis, free association, dreams, and the unconscious, encouraging open interpretation and engaging viewers in a world “like trying to see in the dark.” 
   
 Casey received her MFA from the Rhode Island School of Design. She has had solo exhibitions at Half Gallery, New York; Nino Mier Gallery, Los Angeles; and the Institute for Contemporary Art, Philadelphia. Her work has also appeared in group exhibitions at The Hole, NY; Almine Rech, New York; Blum & Poe, Los Angeles; Gagosian Gallery, New York; Paul Kasmin Gallery, New York; and Nino Mier Gallery, Brussels. 
   
 📅 Art Forum 
 Monday, 4/13/26  5:20pm-7pm 
 Calcia Hall, Room 140 
 Free and open to the public

title: BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voices of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm. 
   
 Titled  AI: Actual Illustrators , the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration.  
   
 Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn. 
   
 Join us for the closing reception to meet and connect with the artists, explore the diverse range of work on view, and celebrate the achievements of this dynamic graduating class. Family, friends, faculty, and the public are warmly invited. 
   
 Mark your calendar and don’t miss this show! 
   
 BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition 
 Exhibition dates: April 9 – April 20, 2026 
 Closing Reception: April 20 from 1:00–5:00pm 
 Finley Gallery, Finley Hall 
 Light refreshments will be served

title: Weekly Food Recovery Initiative
description:
Join our weekly Food Recovery events, where dedicated volunteers and members gather to make a difference in our community. Each week, participants help collect perfectly good leftover food from Gourmet Dining and transport it to Blanton. There, the food is repackaged into individual microwavable containers and stocked in the food pantry, providing meals for those in need throughout the week.  
 To Participate : RSVP to attend the event each week. Meet in Blanton Hall Residence Hall Kitchenette in the Communal Area (Tell them you are working with Food Recovery), Sign in via Event Pass and your set! 
 Requirements: 
 
 Volunteers must be Food Safe Certified to participate. 
 Complete the certification by following the step-by-step instructions provided in this  link . 
 Once certified, email your certification to  foodrecoverynetwork@sgamsu.org  to confirm your eligibility to participate. 
 
 Your involvement helps reduce food waste and insecurity while supporting individuals in our community who are in need. We look forward to your participation! *Please Note: This event end time is dependent on the amount of food given. You can estimate for max of hour and a half for recoveries.

title: 2026 Class Gift Tabling - Information and Philanthropy Cord Pick-Up
description:
Each year, our graduating class participates in a proud Class Gift tradition where members of the class invest in the University by supporting areas of Montclair State University that are most important to them. We invite the Class of 2026 to come together to show your support! 
 All gifts to Montclair count! Make your gift to any area of campus you feel most passionate about – such as the Emergency Book Fund, The Red Hawk Pantry, your school or college, The Fund for Montclair State or athletic program. 
 Your gift will pay it forward for future Red Hawks and kick-start your philanthropic connection to Montclair. 
 
 Commemorate your class year! Make a gift of at least  $20.26  and you’ll receive your customized philanthropy cord to wear over your commencement robe.  
 For a gift of  $40 , celebrate this milestone with your family, friends and colleagues by having your message displayed on the screen at the Commencement you will be participating in, and receive a customized philanthropy cord. 
 
 How do I get my customized philanthropy cord? 
 Customized philanthropy cords will be available for pick-up: 
 
 Tuesday, October 28 from 1:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby 
 Thursday, November 20  from 1:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby 
 Tuesday, December 2 from 12:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby 
 Bloomfield Campus Graduation Fair: Wednesday, December 10th from 12:00 – 6:00 p.m. in Deacon’s Den, Talbott Hall  (for Bloomfield graduates only) 
 Montclair Campus Graduation Fairs: Thursday, December 11th from 10:00 a.m. – 7:00 p.m. in the Student Center Ballrooms  (for Montclair or Bloomfield graduates) 
 
 Graduates can also arrange to pick up their philanthropy cords in the Overlook Corporate Center, 150 Clove Road, Little Falls, NJ during business hours, Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m. Philanthropy cords can also be mailed to those who cannot pick it up. The last day we will mail the cords is Monday, December 22, 2025 at 2:30 p.m. Please contact James Stewart, Associate Director of Annual Giving at stewartj@montclair.edu or 973-655-5328 to finalize options to get your cords for the Winter Commencement. 
 Every student has benefitted from donor generosity to areas of academic support, emergency funding for students and overall priorities to boost students’ success every day. Thank you for paying it forward! For more information, please contact James Stewart, Associate Director of Annual Giving, at 973-655-5328 or stewartj@montclair.edu.

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music. 
 As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge. 
 This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as  tempo, rhythm, and song structure , helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor. 
 Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room.  
 Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves. 
 This class is designed for  beginners and college students , with no prior DJ or music experience required.

This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration.

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Jazz Career Panel
description:
Jazz Career Panel 
 GUESTS 
 Lydia Liebman Seth Abramson Karen Kennedy 
 1-2 pm  - Roundtable Discussion 2-2:30 pm  - Q & A 
 Lydia Liebman Lydia Liebman  is an award-winning jazz media strategist and founder of  Lydia Liebman Promotions , one of the most active and respected PR firms in jazz. Since launching the company from her Emerson College dorm room, she has led more than 800 release campaigns, worked with major jazz artists and institutions, and served on the faculty of both the  Roc Nation School of Music  and  The New School. 
 Seth Abramson Seth Abramson is a Grammy Award–winning producer, guitarist, and entrepreneur. As President of Rabbit Moon Productions, Director of Jazz for The Gilmore, and Senior Artistic Advisor for The Jazz Legacies Fellowship, he helps artists build careers, develop major projects, and navigate the business of music at the highest level. 
 Karen Kennedy Karen Kennedy  is the founder of  24 Seven Artist Development , a full-service management and consulting company that has supported the careers of Grammy-nominated and award-winning artists for more than three decades. A respected arts leader, educator, and advocate, she has held influential roles across the music industry, higher education, and national arts organizations, with a longstanding focus on artist development, access,  and inclusion.

title: BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voices of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm. 
   
 Titled  AI: Actual Illustrators , the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration.  
   
 Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn. 
   
 Join us for the closing reception to meet and connect with the artists, explore the diverse range of work on view, and celebrate the achievements of this dynamic graduating class. Family, friends, faculty, and the public are warmly invited. 
   
 Mark your calendar and don’t miss this show! 
   
 BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition 
 Exhibition dates: April 9 – April 20, 2026 
 Closing Reception: April 20 from 1:00–5:00pm 
 Finley Gallery, Finley Hall 
 Light refreshments will be served

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Flute Masterclass with Jennifer Grim
description:
Flute Masterclass with Jennifer Grim 
 Jennifer Grim,  professor at the  University of Miami,  will present a masterclass for the Cali flute studio, where students will perform and receive individualized feedback. 
 Praised by  The New York Times   as “a deft, smooth flute soloist,”  Jennifer Grim  is a versatile performer whose work spans solo, chamber, and orchestral stages worldwide. 
 A member of the flute-percussion duo  Solidaire  and contemporary ensemble  Flagelot , she has collaborated with leading groups including the  Boston Chamber Music Society  and  Chamber Music Society of Lincoln Center .  Her acclaimed album  T hrough Broken Time  reflects her strong commitment to contemporary music. 
 Grim is Professor and Chair of Instrumental Performance at the  Frost School of Music, University of Miami,  and serves as President of the  National Flute Association Board.

 " And they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers.   And awe came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were being done through the apostles.   And all who believed were together and had all things in common.   And they were selling their possessions and belongings and distributing the proceeds to all, as any had need.   And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts,  praising God and having favor with all the people. title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Tuba Masterclass with Willie Clark
description:
Tuba Masterclass with Willie Clark 
 Willie Clark,   professor of tuba at  University of Maryland  and former tuba with the  United States AIr Force Band   will lead Cali brass students in a masterclass focusing on the fundamentals of expressive brass playing across a landscape of genres. 
 Bio 
 Willie Clark  is a former member of the  United States Air Force Ceremonial Brass  and a founding member of the professional Disney tuba quartet,  The Tubafours.  An internationally active performer, recording artist, and clinician, he has toured extensively throughout the United States, South America, Asia, Australia, and Europe, performing with leading orchestras, ensembles, and popular artists. Clark currently serves on the tuba faculty at the  University of Maryland  and  Howard University,  and is widely respected for his engaging teaching and expressive approach to brass performance.

title: BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voices of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm. 
   
 Titled  AI: Actual Illustrators , the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration.  
   
 Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn. 
   
 Join us for the closing reception to meet and connect with the artists, explore the diverse range of work on view, and celebrate the achievements of this dynamic graduating class. Family, friends, faculty, and the public are warmly invited. 
   
 Mark your calendar and don’t miss this show! 
   
 BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition 
 Exhibition dates: April 9 – April 20, 2026 
 Closing Reception: April 20 from 1:00–5:00pm 
 Finley Gallery, Finley Hall 
 Light refreshments will be served

title: April Coffee House Night RA+P
description:
Coffeehouse is our live music event where students can sign up to perform in Russ Lounge. Whether you’re playing, singing, or just coming to hang out, it’s always a great night of creativity and community. 
 The goal of the event is to turn Russ Lounge into a performance space that can accommodate an audience, but it’s also a hands-on opportunity to learn how to set up a performance from start to finish. We will be mixing on a Midas M32R, so it's also an opportunity to learn how digital mixers for live audio work. All that prep work culminates in a show featuring Montclair State students from 7 PM to 9:15 PM. We go through the process of setup, performance, and breakdown. IF this interests you, please fill out this form for more information on how to join the crew. 
 See you soon, stay groovy!! 
 All students are welcome, commuters need to be signed in a with a resident following Residence Life guest registration procedures.

location: Cream Ridge, NJ - Begins and ends at Student Recreation Center

 Cost for Students - $10 | Faculty/Staff - $20

 10:00 am - 5:00 pm

title: Safe Space Training
description:
Our Safe Space Training is an intensive, interactive training that provides attendees with information and tools to combat homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, cissexism and heterosexism, on campus and beyond. Through interactive activities and discussion, participants will personally reflect on their own experiences to enhance their personal and professional development, provide further education, and finally, identify ways to create change on campus and beyond. 
 All sessions are three hours and are first-come, first-served. Pre-registration is required. Registrants are expected to attend the full session. Only individuals who have fully completed the Safe Space Program and have agreed to the expectations of its membership are able to display the sticker/button of our program. Refreshments are not provided; however, you are more than welcome to bring your lunch, coffee, snacks, etc.

title: Closing reception for the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the Spring 2026 BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voice of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm. 
   
 Titled  AI: Actual Illustrators , the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration.  
   
 Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn. 
   
 Join us for the closing reception to meet and connect with the artists, explore the diverse range of work on view, and celebrate the achievements of this dynamic graduating class. Family, friends, faculty, and the public are warmly invited. 
   
 Mark your calendar and don’t miss this show! 
   
 BFA Illustration Thesis  Exhibition 
 Closing Reception: April 20 from 1:00–5:00pm 
 Exhibition dates: April 9 – April 20, 2026 
 Finley Gallery, Finley Hall 
 Light refreshments will be served

 This event is open to all—men and women, no matter where you are in your faith. Whether you’re strong in your walk, still growing, or just curious, you are welcome here.

 Come with an open heart, ready to listen, connect, and experience a night of real stories, real people, and a real God.

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music. 
 As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge. 
 This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as  tempo, rhythm, and song structure , helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor. 
 Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room.  
 Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves. 
 This class is designed for  beginners and college students , with no prior DJ or music experience required.

title: Why Godzilla Still Matters: Montclair State Professor Jason Abbott to Speak in Brooklyn
organization: College of Humanities and Social Sciences (CHSS)
description:
Why Godzilla Still Matters: Montclair State Professor Jason Abbott to Speak in Brooklyn 
 Dr. Jason Abbott, Professor of Political Science and International Relations at Montclair State University, will deliver a public lecture as part of the Lectures on Tap series in Brooklyn, New York. 
   
 Title: Godzilla:  Japan, Trauma, and the Unkillable Monster Date:  Wednesday, April 22, 2026 Time:  6:30 p.m. – 8:30 p.m. (Lecture begins at 7:00 p.m.) Location:  Bushwick, Brooklyn, NY (exact address provided upon registration) Please Register:   https://www.eventbrite.com/e/lectures-on-tap-godzilla-japan-trauma-and-the-unkillable-monster-tickets-1985135400421?aff=oddtdtcreator 
   
 Lectures on Tap is a public lecture series that brings professors, experts, and storytellers into informal venues to deliver accessible, thought-provoking talks. 
   
 In this lecture, Dr. Abbott examines Godzilla as a cultural lens through which to understand Japan’s postwar experience. Emerging in the shadow of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, Godzilla has long served as a cinematic reflection of national trauma, nuclear anxiety, and unresolved historical memory. The talk explores why the figure of Godzilla reappears at moments of crisis and what this enduring monster reveals about how societies process catastrophe and construct collective memory. 
   
 Drawing on examples across multiple films and historical periods, the lecture considers how trauma is not simply resolved, but often reworked and reimagined over time. 
 Agenda: 6:30 p.m. – Doors open; seating, food, and drinks available 6:55 p.m. – Host introduction 7:00 p.m. – Lecture begins 7:45 p.m. – Audience Q&A 8:00 p.m. – Informal discussion and networking 8:30 p.m. – Event concludes

Attendees will reflect on the many roles they navigate in their academic and personal lives and explore healthy ways to manage stress, prioritize themselves, and support one another.

title: Cru Large Group!
description:
Cru is a caring community passionate about connecting people to Jesus Christ!  Join us in learning together on Who Christ is, building a personal relationship with Him, and joining a community filled with love and open arms!  
 This semester, we will be focusing on the theme  "Identity in Christ."  We pray that our students will learn more about who they are in Christ, and look at some examples of people in the Bible where God revealed their true identity in Christ! 
 " And they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers.   And awe came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were being done through the apostles.   And all who believed were together and had all things in common.   And they were selling their possessions and belongings and distributing the proceeds to all, as any had need.   And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts,  praising God and having favor with all the people. And the Lord added to their number day by day those who were being saved." - Acts 2:42-47

title: Free Family Swim Night
description:
Campus Recreation invites you to bring your family (up to 4 guests) to Free Family Swim Night on Friday, April 24 from 5:00-9:30 p.m. at the Student Recreation Center Pool. You and your family will have FREE use of our pool and locker rooms (no membership to the facility is required). Flotation devices are allowed as long as they are USCG approved. We have life jackets available - just ask the lifeguard. 
   
 Please note the following: 
 
 Bring your Montclair ID in order to check-in. 
 No prior registration required. 
 You must accompany your family or guest(s) at all times during the program. 
 All children must be over the age of 5 and fully potty-trained. 
 
 When and Where? 
 
 Saturday, April 24, 2026 
 5:00 pm - 9:45 pm 
 Student Recreation Center - Pool  
 
 Email us at srcaquatics@montclair.edu with any questions.  We look forward to seeing you at the pool!

title: Black River (Easy)
description:
Join us for a gorgeous hike through Hacklebarney state park. The trail features the Black River as a centerpiece and is quite stunning in the spring season. 
   
 How do I sign-up? 
 
 Register in the Student Recreation Center main office or over the phone (973-655-3340). 
 Cost for Students $10 | Faculty/Staff/Community $20 
 
   
 Where and when? 
 
 Sunday, April 26th, 2026 
 10am-4pm  
 Bus leaves from Student Recreation Center 
 
   
 What to bring:  Water, Lunch, and Boots or Sneakers 
   
 Potential Hazards:  Unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, limited vision, dehydration, allergic reactions/poisonous plants, insect bites, and injury by running, including sprains, lacerations, abrasions and fractures.

title: Mother's Day Brunch
description:
Join us for a heartfelt and elegant  Mother’s Day Brunch  as we celebrate the incredible women who nurture, support, and inspire us every day. This special gathering is dedicated to honoring mothers and mother figures with good food, great company, and meaningful moments. 
 Enjoy a delicious brunch spread in a warm and welcoming atmosphere filled with music, laughter, and love. Whether you’re attending with your mom, a loved one, or coming to celebrate the community, this event is all about appreciation and connection.

title: Caribbean Student Organization Carnival

Enjoy high-energy soca, reggae, and dancehall beats while tasting delicious Caribbean foods like jerk chicken and empanadas. Experience colorful costumes, lively performances, and fun activities that bring the spirit of the islands to campus. Grab your friends, immerse yourself in the culture, and don’t miss this unforgettable celebration of Caribbean heritage and community!

title: Miles Without Borders
description:
Miles Without Borders is a high-energy, Olympic-style field day bringing together fun, fitness, and philanthropy! In collaboration with MSU Run for Fun, Friends of Doctors Without Borders (FOMSF) invites students to compete in exciting team-based challenges, races, and mini-games designed for all skill levels. 
 This event isn’t just about winning—it’s about making a difference. As part of our initiative, we are hosting a  food drive  to support local communities in need. Participants and attendees are encouraged to bring non-perishable food items to contribute to the cause. 
 Come out to run, play, and give back while supporting global medical humanitarian efforts. Whether you're competing or cheering from the sidelines, your participation helps make an impact both locally and worldwide. 
 Let’s move with purpose and turn friendly competition into meaningful change!

title: Denim Day 2026: Mind the Gaps, Move Forward
description:
April is Sexual Assault Awareness Month, and together, we can break the silence.  The Montclair State University Police Department and the Office of Advocacy & Outreach are committed to fostering a campus where survivors feel heard, supported, and empowered.  It starts with believing. It starts with you.  By offering a safe and affirming response to those who disclose gender-based and sexual violence, we can create lasting change. Let’s stand together to end the cycle of silence—because  every voice matters, and every action counts. 
 Denim Day is recognized internationally as a day of action and awareness, encouraging people to wear denim to challenge victim-blaming and educate others about sexual violence.  This year, Denim Day falls on Wednesday, April 29, 2026. 
 The University Police and  Office of Advocacy & Outreach and the Office of Advocacy and Community Resilience , in partnership with over 30 campus and local community organizations, invites you to join us  from 9 a.m. to 12 p.m. at the Amphitheater  for a powerful awareness event honoring survivors and increasing awareness of available resources. 
 Despite the  gaps  that still exist in the criminal justice system, in resources, and in prevention education,  we will move forward together  as a community. As part of this commitment, we will walk  one mile around campus in solidarity with survivors.

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Graduate Conducting Recital Night
description:
Graduate Conducting Recital Night 
 
 
   
 This recital highlights the artistry and individuality of emerging conductors  Gary Blackman,  Tristan Hughes,  and  Alec Hamilton,   each leading chamber groups in a diverse program. The performance offers a compelling look at the craft of conducting, showcasing their interpretive insight, leadership, and collaborative musicianship through performances of Copland's  Appalachian Spring , Barber's  Knoxville Summer of 1915  and his  Adagio for Strings ,  as well as works by Mozart and Beethoven.

title: Campus Earth Month
description:
Participate in Campus Earth Month throughout April by collecting code words and earning entries to win prizes. Each code word you collect or action you complete will earn an entry into the Campus Earth Month Raffle. The more entries you earn, the better your odds. 
 Each decision you make has the potential to be good for the planet and people. No one can do it all, but we can all do something. This April, make sure your ledger is balanced so you are always…+1 for the Planet, People, Yourself, & Montclair! 
 Your goal is to collect as many code words as you can throughout April. At every in-person Earth Month event, a code word will be available. It will either be displayed at the event or communicated to you during the event verbally. When the event concludes, you can also ask the organizer for the code word. 
 Sustainability Student Interns will be out on campus with opportunities to earn additional code words and code words will also be available virtually. 
 You are responsible for keeping track of your code words while participating and  submitting them all in one email to   sustainability@montclair.edu  by April, 30 2026. 
 Each decision you make has the potential to be good for the planet. No one can do it all, but we can all do something. This April, make sure your ledger is balanced so you are always…+1 for the Planet, People, Yourself, & Montclair! ❤️🌎😊🦅🐻 Full details, including additional Raffle entry options, a complete prize list, & rules are available at: https://www.montclair.edu/sustainability/earth-month/ For a full-list of events visit here: https://www.montclair.edu/sustainability/earth-month-events/

title: Visit the University Galleries' Spring 2026 Exhibition! Carpe Diem: Select Alumni in the Visual Arts
location: George Segal Gallery, 4th Floor of the Red Hawk Deck
description:
On view from February 3 – May 3, 2026 in the George Segal Gallery. Public hours are Tuesday – Friday, 12 to 4 pm. 
   
 Admission is free, open to the public.  Learn more about this exhibition 
   
 Carpe Diem: Select Alumni in the Visual Arts  is a group exhibition presented by the Office for Development and Alumni Engagement and hosted by the University Galleries.  Carpe Diem  brings together twelve accomplished alumni working in a range of media. 
   
 Curated by Sally Morgan Lehman, who has taught in the Department of Art and Design at Montclair and is owner and director of Morgan Lehman Gallery in New York City,  Carpe Diem  highlights the breadth of artistic practices fostered at Montclair and the lasting impact of its visual arts alumni. 
   
 Featured artists include Flávia Berindoague ’13, Bisa Butler ’05, Jonathan Calm ’97, Kyle Coniglio ’10, Alyssa Fanning ’12, Allen Ginsberg (1943), Márta Kucsora (2005–06), Michael Manning ’11, Nancy Bergman Pantirer ’80, Tiffany Perez ’09, William Pope.L ’78, and Maria Valdivia ’24.

title: CSAM Science Teaching Program - Program Meeting
description:
The  CSAM Science Teaching Program  holds  biweekly meetings  that are open, welcoming, and inclusive! Any student curious about science teaching is invited to join us. Each meeting highlights a theme related to science education, from classroom strategies to career pathways, and offers students opportunities to connect with mentors, explore internships, and gain real-world experience working with teachers and children. Students are encouraged to  bring a guest  who may also be interested in exploring science teaching. 
 These gatherings help us build a strong  community of support , where students encourage one another, share experiences, and access valuable opportunities such as  scholarships, paid internships, and mentoring  from advanced peers and in-service science teachers. Whether you are just beginning to explore the idea of teaching or already on the path toward certification, these meetings are a place to grow, learn, and belong.

title: Safe Space Training
description:
Our Safe Space Training is an intensive, interactive training that provides attendees with information and tools to combat homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, cissexism and heterosexism, on campus and beyond. Through interactive activities and discussion, participants will personally reflect on their own experiences to enhance their personal and professional development, provide further education, and finally, identify ways to create change on campus and beyond. 
 All sessions are three hours and are first-come, first-served. Pre-registration is required. Registrants are expected to attend the full session. Only individuals who have fully completed the Safe Space Program and have agreed to the expectations of its membership are able to display the sticker/button of our program. Refreshments are not provided; however, you are more than welcome to bring your lunch, coffee, snacks, etc.
